reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) was an emergency loan facility that ceased making new loans in March 2024. All loans had a one-year term limit, and as expected, the balance reached exactly 0.0 by late March 2025. It has remained at 0.0 every week since then. Because the facility is closed and fully wound down, there is no mechanism for new loans to be added to this specific program. Reopening the exact same facility under the same FRED series would require a new banking crisis and a decision to revive this previously criticized structure (rather than creating a new one), which is highly improbable. Thus, the probability of the balance increasing above 0.0 is exceptionally close to zero across all time horizons. Probabilities reflect the system minimum (0.02) for the nearest dates, increasing just slightly for a six-month horizon simply to reflect irreducible uncertainty.
